execution_summary: Built a Python 3.10-slim Docker image with pandas, numpy, statsmodels, and scipy. Initial build failed due to shell parsing of version constraints; fixed by pinning exact versions in replication_info.json and regenerating the Dockerfile. Started the container, then executed the Python data-prep entrypoint which chains into the analysis. Addressed path issues by adjusting the volume mount to map the study root to /app/data and rewriting the scripts to search multiple data paths and preserve needed columns. Resolved a missing statsmodels import at runtime by enhancing the analysis script to install dependencies to the user site and add site-packages to sys.path. The final run completed successfully, estimating the hierarchical OLS on the processed dataset. The focal predictor (req_relax) was positive and met the preregistered criterion for one-tailed significance (two-tailed p = 0.085 ≈ one-tailed p ≈ 0.0425).

code_executed.logs: FileNotFoundError: /app/data/replication_data/Raw data/k17_t1_data.csv. After fixing, KeyError: 'StartDate_dt' during wave computation. Then ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'statsmodels' when chaining to analysis.
code_executed.environment: Docker image (python:3.10-slim) with intended deps; initial runtime missing statsmodels import for the non-root user context.

code_executed.logs: Processed data already available at: /workspace/replication_data/k17_processed_data.csv. Skipping data prep and proceeding to analysis. Executing analysis script at: /workspace/replication_data/k17_analysis__py.py. Results: unstandardized coef(req_relax)=0.0717, p=0.0851; standardized coef(req_relax)=0.0982, p=0.0851.
code_executed.environment: Python 3.10 with pandas 1.5.3, numpy 1.24.4, statsmodels 0.14.0, scipy 1.10.1 inside the container (statsmodels ensured via user-site installation and sys.path adjustments).

results.hypothesis_tested: Among preschool teachers, weekend relaxation at Time 2 (Sunday night) positively predicts joviality at Time 3 (following Friday), controlling for T1 joviality, other weekend recovery experiences (detachment, mastery, control), non-work hassles, and demographics.

findings_summary.outcome_name: Unstandardized coefficient for req_relax in full model
Standardized coefficient for req_relax in full model

tables.table_description: JSON summary of hierarchical OLS results, including focal coefficient for req_relax (unstandardized and standardized), p-values, R-squared, and N.
